alinia.description: Which detections to run and their thresholds. Pass either a detection-configuration ID string registered with Alinia, or a dict enabling detections inline — e.g. ``{"security": True}``, or nested per-policy settings such as ``{"safety": {"toxicity": 0.8}}``.
alinia.name: detection_config

alinia.description: Alinia API endpoint URL (obtained from Alinia alongside the key). If ``None``, it is read from the ``ALINIA_ENDPOINT`` environment variable.
alinia.name: endpoint

alinia.description: Optional metadata dict sent with every request (e.g. app or user identifiers for Alinia-side monitoring).
alinia.name: metadata

alinia.description: Optional response Alinia should return when content is blocked, e.g. ``{"output": "Sorry, I can't help with that."}``.
alinia.name: blocked_response

alinia.description: Whether to request a streaming API response. Defaults to ``False``.
alinia.name: stream

alinia.description: Optional model response to validate alongside the input, for detections that evaluate outputs (e.g. hallucination or compliance checks).
alinia.name: output

alinia.description: Optional context documents (e.g. retrieved RAG passages) that give output-side detections the grounding text to check against.
alinia.name: context_documents

any_llm.description: Natural-language policy to validate against, e.g. ``"The text must not request or contain personal data."``. Substituted into the system prompt's ``{policy}`` placeholder.
any_llm.name: policy

any_llm.description: The judge model in any-llm's ``provider:model`` format, e.g. ``"openai:gpt-5-nano"`` (default) or ``"mistral:mistral-small-latest"``. The model must support structured output.
any_llm.name: model_id

any_llm.description: Override the system prompt. Defaults to ``None``, which uses the registry default (:data:`DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT`) — or the version selected by ``prompt_version``. A supplied prompt should have a ``{policy}`` placeholder and instruct the model to return the ``valid`` / ``explanation`` / ``risk_score`` fields of :class:`GuardrailOutputAnyLLM`.
any_llm.name: system_prompt

any_llm.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``system_prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See :meth:`any_guardrail.AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions`.
any_llm.name: prompt_version

azure_content_safety.description: The endpoint URL for the Azure Content Safety service.
azure_content_safety.name: endpoint

azure_content_safety.description: The threshold for determining if content is unsafe.
azure_content_safety.name: threshold

azure_content_safety.description: The type of score to use ("max" or "avg").
azure_content_safety.name: score_type

azure_content_safety.description: List of blocklist names to use for content evaluation.
azure_content_safety.name: blocklist_names

azure_prompt_shields.description: Azure Content Safety endpoint URL. If ``None``, the value is read from the ``CONTENT_SAFETY_ENDPOINT`` environment variable.
azure_prompt_shields.name: endpoint

azure_prompt_shields.description: Auxiliary documents (e.g. retrieved context, tool outputs) to scan for indirect (data-borne) prompt-injection. If ``None``, only the user prompt is analyzed.
azure_prompt_shields.name: documents

bedrock_guardrails.description: The AWS guardrail identifier (ID or ARN) of an existing Bedrock Guardrail. Create one in the Bedrock console before instantiating this class.
bedrock_guardrails.name: guardrail_identifier

bedrock_guardrails.description: The guardrail version to apply. ``"DRAFT"`` refers to the working draft; otherwise pass a published numeric version string (e.g. ``"1"``).
bedrock_guardrails.name: guardrail_version

bedrock_guardrails.description: Either ``"INPUT"`` (apply policy to a user prompt) or ``"OUTPUT"`` (apply policy to a model response). Validated on construction.
bedrock_guardrails.name: source

bedrock_guardrails.description: Optional AWS region (e.g. ``"us-east-1"``). Falls back to the boto3 default chain when ``None``.
bedrock_guardrails.name: region_name

bedrock_guardrails.description: Optional explicit access key. Prefer environment variables or an IAM role in production.
bedrock_guardrails.name: aws_access_key_id

bedrock_guardrails.description: Optional explicit secret key. Prefer environment variables or an IAM role in production.
bedrock_guardrails.name: aws_secret_access_key

bedrock_guardrails.description: Optional pre-configured ``boto3.Session`` instance. When supplied, takes precedence over the explicit credentials and region_name kwargs.
bedrock_guardrails.name: boto3_session

bielik_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``speakleash/Bielik-Guard-0.1B-v1.1``. Pass ``speakleash/Bielik-Guard-0.5B-v1.1`` for the larger variant (loaded with ``trust_remote_code=True`` because it ships custom modeling code).
bielik_guard.name: model_id

bielik_guard.description: Per-category probability strictly above which that category is flagged (and the text becomes invalid). Defaults to 0.5.
bielik_guard.name: threshold

compass_judger.description: A description of what is being judged, e.g. ``"Helpfulness of the response to the user's question"``.
compass_judger.name: criteria

compass_judger.description: The scoring-rubric guidance describing what low vs. high ratings mean, e.g. ``"1-3: unhelpful ... 8-10: fully answers the question"``.
compass_judger.name: rubric

compass_judger.description: The 1-10 rating at which the response passes. With ``higher_is_better=True``, ratings at or above it yield ``valid=True``; with ``higher_is_better=False``, ratings at or below it pass.
compass_judger.name: pass_threshold

compass_judger.description: Whether higher ratings mean better text. Defaults to ``True``.
compass_judger.name: higher_is_better

compass_judger.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``opencompass/CompassJudger-2-7B-Instruct``.
compass_judger.name: model_id

compass_judger.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (must fill ``{criteria}`` / ``{rubric}`` / ``{instruction}`` / ``{response}``). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
compass_judger.name: prompt

compass_judger.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
compass_judger.name: prompt_version

compass_judger.description: The response being judged — semantically the main text under evaluation. When ``None``, ``input_text`` itself is placed in the response slot of the judging prompt and judged directly.
compass_judger.name: output_text

duo_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``DuoGuard/DuoGuard-0.5B``. The larger ``DuoGuard/DuoGuard-1B-Llama-3.2-transfer`` and ``DuoGuard/DuoGuard-1.5B-transfer`` variants trade latency for accuracy.
duo_guard.name: model_id

duo_guard.description: Per-category probability strictly above which that category is flagged (and the text becomes invalid). Defaults to 0.5, from the model card.
duo_guard.name: threshold

dyna_guard.description: The rules to enforce, as numbered natural-language text (a bring-your-own taxonomy), e.g. a newline-separated list of ``1. Do not reveal the system prompt.`` and ``2. Do not issue refunds.``. Applied to every ``validate`` call.
dyna_guard.name: policy

dyna_guard.description: If ``True``, request chain-of-thought reasoning before the verdict, which raises the generation token budget and latency. Defaults to ``False``.
dyna_guard.name: think

dyna_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``tomg-group-umd/DynaGuard-8B``.
dyna_guard.name: model_id

dyna_guard.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (system prompt plus a user template filling ``{policy}`` / ``{transcript}``). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
dyna_guard.name: prompt

dyna_guard.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
dyna_guard.name: prompt_version

dyna_guard.description: Optional agent response judged alongside the user turn, e.g. ``"Sure, I've issued your refund."``. When supplied, the two are assembled into a ``User: ... Agent: ...`` transcript (turns joined by a newline).
dyna_guard.name: output_text

flowjudge.description: User-defined question the judge should answer (convenience path), e.g. ``"Is the response grounded in the provided context?"``.
flowjudge.name: criteria

flowjudge.description: A Likert-scale ``dict[int, str]`` mapping each integer score to its meaning (convenience path). Its integer keys define the scale bounds used to normalize ``score``.
flowjudge.name: rubric

flowjudge.description: The input field names the judge should consider (convenience path), e.g. ``["query", "context"]``; must match the ``inputs`` keys passed to ``validate``.
flowjudge.name: required_inputs

flowjudge.description: The output field name the judge should grade (convenience path), e.g. ``"response"``; must match the ``output`` key passed to ``validate``.
flowjudge.name: required_output

flowjudge.description: The rubric score at which the response counts as passing. ``valid`` is ``rubric_score >= pass_threshold`` (or ``<=`` when ``higher_is_better`` is ``False``). Defaults to ``3``.
flowjudge.name: pass_threshold

flowjudge.description: Whether higher rubric scores mean better responses. Set ``False`` for rubrics where a higher number is worse. Defaults to ``True``.
flowjudge.name: higher_is_better

flowjudge.description: A prebuilt ``flow_judge`` ``Metric`` / ``CustomMetric`` or preset (e.g. ``RESPONSE_FAITHFULNESS_3POINT``). When given, the convenience fields are ignored. Keyword-only.
flowjudge.name: metric

flowjudge.description: A prebuilt ``flow_judge`` backend (``Hf``, ``Vllm``, ``Llamafile``, ``Baseten``). Defaults to ``Hf(flash_attn=False)``. Install the matching ``flow-judge[vllm|llamafile|baseten]`` extra yourself for non-default backends. Keyword-only.
flowjudge.name: model

flowjudge.description: Generation parameters (``temperature``, ``top_p``, ``max_new_tokens``, ``do_sample``) for the default ``Hf`` backend; ignored when ``model`` is supplied. Keyword-only.
flowjudge.name: generation_params

flowjudge.description: A single-key dictionary mapping the ``required_output`` name to the text being graded, e.g. ``{"response": "The capital of France is Paris."}``.
flowjudge.name: output

gli_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``fastino/gliguard-LLMGuardrails-300M``.
gli_guard.name: model_id

gli_guard.description: Classification threshold forwarded to ``gliner2``'s ``classify_text`` for the whole schema. Defaults to 0.5; the toxicity task overrides it with its own ``cls_threshold`` of 0.4 in ``GLIGUARD_SCHEMA``.
gli_guard.name: threshold

gli_ner_pii.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``fastino/gliner2-privacy-filter-PII-multi``.
gli_ner_pii.name: model_id

gli_ner_pii.description: Default confidence threshold forwarded to ``gliner2``'s ``extract_entities``. Defaults to 0.5; a per-call ``threshold`` overrides it.
gli_ner_pii.name: threshold

gli_ner_pii.description: PII entity types to detect. Defaults to ``DEFAULT_PII_ENTITIES``.
gli_ner_pii.name: entity_types

gli_ner_pii.description: Confidence threshold for this call. Defaults to the instance ``threshold``.
gli_ner_pii.name: threshold

gli_ner_pii.description: Template used to replace each detected span in ``modified_text``; the substring ``{label}`` is replaced with the upper-cased entity type (e.g. ``"[REDACTED_EMAIL]"``). Any other braces are kept literal, so an arbitrary placeholder never raises.
gli_ner_pii.name: redaction_placeholder

glider.description: A question or description of what is being judged, e.g. ``"Is the response free of unsupported medical claims?"``.
glider.name: pass_criteria

glider.description: A free-text scoring rubric telling the model what each score means, e.g. ``"0: contains unsupported claims. 1: all claims are supported."``.
glider.name: rubric

glider.description: The rubric score at which the text counts as passing. ``valid`` is ``rubric_score >= pass_threshold`` (or ``<=`` when ``higher_is_better=False``). Must be on the same scale as the rubric.
glider.name: pass_threshold

glider.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``PatronusAI/glider``.
glider.name: model_id

glider.description: Whether higher rubric scores mean better/passing text. Set to ``False`` for rubrics where higher scores mean worse text (e.g. a severity scale).
glider.name: higher_is_better

glider.description: Optional ``(min, max)`` bounds of the rubric scale, e.g. ``(0, 1)`` or ``(1, 5)``. Supplying it enables the normalized canonical risk in ``GuardrailOutput.score``; when omitted, ``score`` is ``None`` and the raw rubric value is still available in ``extra["rubric_score"]``.
glider.name: score_range

glider.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (system prompt filling ``{data}`` / ``{pass_criteria}`` / ``{rubric}`` plus the ``input`` / ``input_output`` data wrappers). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
glider.name: prompt

glider.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
glider.name: prompt_version

glider.description: Optional second text, wrapped in ``<OUTPUT>`` tags and judged alongside ``input_text`` — typically the model response when the pass criteria compare a response against a prompt (e.g. ``"Does the OUTPUT answer the question in the INPUT?"``).
glider.name: output_text

gpt_oss_safeguard.description: The written safety policy (bring-your-own-taxonomy) the model evaluates content against — e.g. a numbered list of disallowed-content rules with definitions and examples. It is installed as the system message, with a short output instruction appended so the model ends its reply with ``VIOLATION`` or ``SAFE``.
gpt_oss_safeguard.name: policy

gpt_oss_safeguard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``: ``openai/gpt-oss-safeguard-20b`` (default) or ``openai/gpt-oss-safeguard-120b``.
gpt_oss_safeguard.name: model_id

granite_guardian.description: The judging criterion applied to every ``validate`` call. Use a :class:`GraniteGuardianRisk` constant (e.g. ``GraniteGuardianRisk.HARM``, ``GraniteGuardianRisk.GROUNDEDNESS``) or a custom Bring-Your-Own-Criteria string. Phrase it as a violation (``"the text contains harmful content"``) so ``valid=True`` means the criterion was *not* met.
granite_guardian.name: criteria

granite_guardian.description: If ``True``, run in think mode — the model emits a ``<think>...</think>`` chain-of-thought block before the ``<score>`` verdict (up to 2048 new tokens, higher latency). Defaults to ``False``, which returns the verdict immediately (up to 48 new tokens).
granite_guardian.name: think

granite_guardian.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``ibm-granite/granite-guardian-4.1-8b``.
granite_guardian.name: model_id

granite_guardian.description: Optional assistant response. Required for criteria that judge the assistant (e.g. groundedness, answer relevance, function-call hallucination); omit to judge the user input directly (e.g. jailbreak, harm, context relevance).
granite_guardian.name: output_text

granite_guardian.description: Optional RAG documents (dicts with ``doc_id`` and ``text``). Required for groundedness and context-relevance criteria.
granite_guardian.name: documents

granite_guardian.description: Optional tool definitions (dicts with ``name``, ``description``, ``parameters``). Required for function-call hallucination checks.
granite_guardian.name: available_tools

harm_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``hbseong/HarmAug-Guard``.
harm_guard.name: model_id

harm_guard.description: Unsafe-probability cutoff at or above which the input is flagged unsafe (``valid=False``). Defaults to 0.5, the value used in the HarmAug paper; lower it to catch borderline content, raise it to reduce false positives.
harm_guard.name: threshold

harm_guard.description: Optional model response. When provided, ``input_text`` and ``output_text`` are tokenized as a text pair so the response is judged for safety in the context of the prompt; when ``None``, only the prompt is judged.
harm_guard.name: output_text

injec_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``leolee99/PIGuard`` (the maintained successor). Pass ``"leolee99/InjecGuard"`` for the original repository, kept for backward compatibility.
injec_guard.name: model_id

jasper.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``JasperLS/gelectra-base-injection`` (a German-language gELECTRA encoder). Pass ``"JasperLS/deberta-v3-base-injection"`` for the English DeBERTa-v3 variant.
jasper.name: model_id

kanana_safeguard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS`` (``kakaocorp/kanana-safeguard-8b``, ``kakaocorp/kanana-safeguard-siren-8b``, ``kakaocorp/kanana-safeguard-prompt-2.1b``). Defaults to the harm model ``kakaocorp/kanana-safeguard-8b``. Each variant carries its own unsafe-code taxonomy (see the class docstring).
kanana_safeguard.name: model_id

kanana_safeguard.description: Optional assistant response. Only the harm model (``kakaocorp/kanana-safeguard-8b``) is trained to judge an assistant turn; the ``-siren-8b`` and ``-prompt-2.1b`` variants silently ignore this argument.
kanana_safeguard.name: output_text

lakera_guard.description: Lakera Guard API endpoint URL. Defaults to the v2 endpoint at ``https://api.lakera.ai/v2/guard``; override for self-hosted or regional deployments.
lakera_guard.name: endpoint

lakera_guard.description: Optional Lakera project ID (e.g. ``"project-1234"``). Projects carry per-project policy configuration (which detectors run, severity thresholds, custom rules); when supplied, it is forwarded with each request so that project's policy is applied.
lakera_guard.name: project_id

lakera_guard.description: If ``True`` (default), request the per-detector ``breakdown`` list, which also enables the graded ``score`` / ``categories`` mapping. If ``False``, ``score`` degrades to ``1.0``/``0.0`` and ``categories`` is empty.
lakera_guard.name: breakdown

lakera_guard.description: If ``True`` (default), request the ``payload`` list locating PII / profanity / custom-regex matches (``start`` / ``end`` offsets, matched ``text``, ``labels``), surfaced in ``extra["payload"]``.
lakera_guard.name: payload

lakera_guard.description: If ``True``, request Lakera build information (git revision, model version) in the response, surfaced in ``extra["dev_info"]``. Defaults to ``False``.
lakera_guard.name: dev_info

lakera_guard.description: Optional request metadata forwarded to Lakera for observability, e.g. ``{"user_id": "u-42", "session_id": "s-1"}``.
lakera_guard.name: metadata

lettuce_detect.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS`` (``KRLabsOrg/lettucedect-base-modernbert-en-v1`` or ``KRLabsOrg/lettucedect-large-modernbert-en-v1``). Defaults to the base model; the large model trades speed for accuracy.
lettuce_detect.name: model_id

lettuce_detect.description: Detection method forwarded to ``lettucedetect``'s ``HallucinationDetector`` constructor. Defaults to ``"transformer"``, the encoder token-classification method the supported checkpoints were trained for.
lettuce_detect.name: method

lettuce_detect.description: The grounding context (a string or list of strings). Required.
lettuce_detect.name: context

lettuce_detect.description: Optional question the answer responds to.
lettuce_detect.name: question

llama_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Selects the variant — ``meta-llama/Llama-Guard-3-1B`` (default) or ``meta-llama/Llama-Guard-3-8B`` for Llama Guard 3, ``meta-llama/Llama-Guard-4-12B`` for Llama Guard 4.
llama_guard.name: model_id

llama_guard.description: Optional assistant response. When provided, an ``assistant`` turn is appended so Llama Guard judges the response in the context of the prompt.
llama_guard.name: output_text

nemotron_content_safety.description: If ``True``, request chain-of-thought reasoning (``/think``) before the verdict; otherwise ``/no_think``. Slower but can improve borderline judgments; the reasoning is stripped before parsing but kept in ``GuardrailOutput.explanation``.
nemotron_content_safety.name: think

nemotron_content_safety.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``nvidia/Nemotron-Content-Safety-Reasoning-4B``.
nemotron_content_safety.name: model_id

nemotron_content_safety.description: Optional assistant response moderated alongside the prompt. When provided, a missing or unparsable response verdict causes the guardrail to fail closed.
nemotron_content_safety.name: output_text

off_topic.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID choosing which detector to load. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``: ``mozilla-ai/jina-embeddings-v2-small-en-off-topic`` loads the Jina bi-encoder (``OffTopicJina``); ``mozilla-ai/stsb-roberta-base-off-topic`` (the default) loads the STSB cross-encoder (``OffTopicStsb``).
off_topic.name: model_id

off_topic.description: The reference topic to compare against — typically the system prompt or the app's intended subject, e.g. ``"You are a customer-support bot for an online bookstore."``. Although it defaults to ``None`` for signature reasons, it is semantically required: a missing or empty value raises ``ValueError``.
off_topic.name: comparison_text

openai_moderation.description: The moderation model to use. Defaults to ``omni-moderation-latest``.
openai_moderation.name: model_id

openai_moderation.description: Optional custom base URL for the OpenAI client (useful for proxies or Azure-style routing).
openai_moderation.name: base_url

openai_moderation.description: Maximum per-category score above which content is considered invalid even if OpenAI did not explicitly flag it. Defaults to ``0.5``.
openai_moderation.name: threshold

pangolin.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``dcarpintero/pangolin-guard-base`` (ModernBERT-base). Pass ``"dcarpintero/pangolin-guard-large"`` for the higher-accuracy ModernBERT-large variant with an 8192-token context. Both share the same ``"unsafe"`` label.
pangolin.name: model_id

patronus.description: The evaluators to run, each a dict with at least an ``"evaluator"`` key plus optional ``"criteria"`` / ``"explain_strategy"``. Example: ``[{"evaluator": "judge", "criteria": "patronus:prompt-injection"}]``. Must be non-empty.
patronus.name: evaluators

patronus.description: Evaluate API endpoint URL. Defaults to ``https://api.patronus.ai/v1/evaluate``.
patronus.name: endpoint

patronus.description: How to combine multiple evaluators into the overall ``valid`` verdict — ``"all_pass"`` (every evaluator must pass) or ``"any_pass"`` (at least one must). Defaults to ``"all_pass"``.
patronus.name: success_strategy

patronus.description: Optional tags forwarded with each request for observability, e.g. ``{"env": "prod"}``.
patronus.name: tags

patronus.description: The model output to evaluate. Required by evaluators that judge a response (e.g. hallucination, answer relevance).
patronus.name: output_text

patronus.description: RAG context document(s). Required by grounding / hallucination evaluators.
patronus.name: retrieved_context

poly_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``ToxicityPrompts/PolyGuard-Ministral``; ``ToxicityPrompts/PolyGuard-Qwen`` and ``ToxicityPrompts/PolyGuard-Qwen-Smol`` are the Qwen-based alternatives.
poly_guard.name: model_id

poly_guard.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (system prompt plus a user template filling ``{prompt}`` / ``{response}``). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
poly_guard.name: prompt

poly_guard.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
poly_guard.name: prompt_version

poly_guard.description: Optional assistant response, judged for harm and refusal alongside the request. When omitted, the response slot is left empty.
poly_guard.name: output_text

prometheus.description: The score rubric applied to every ``validate`` call — the evaluation criteria plus ``Score 1:`` … ``Score 5:`` descriptions, e.g. ``"Is the answer factually correct? Score 1: entirely wrong. ... Score 5: fully correct."``.
prometheus.name: rubric

prometheus.description: The score (1-5) at or above which the response passes (or at or below when ``higher_is_better=False``), e.g. ``4``.
prometheus.name: pass_threshold

prometheus.description: Optional gold answer that would earn a score of 5, supplied to the model as an anchor for the top of the scale. Defaults to ``None`` (no reference; an empty string is sent).
prometheus.name: reference_answer

prometheus.description: Whether higher rubric scores mean better responses. Set ``False`` for rubrics where a higher number is worse (e.g. a severity scale). Defaults to ``True``.
prometheus.name: higher_is_better

prometheus.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``prometheus-eval/prometheus-7b-v2.0``.
prometheus.name: model_id

prometheus.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (must fill ``{instruction}`` / ``{response}`` / ``{reference_answer}`` / ``{rubric}``). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
prometheus.name: prompt

prometheus.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
prometheus.name: prompt_version

prometheus.description: The response being graded against the rubric, e.g. ``"The sky is blue because sunlight scatters off the air."``. When ``None``, ``input_text`` itself is graded as the response.
prometheus.name: output_text

prompt_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``meta-llama/Llama-Prompt-Guard-2-86M`` (mDeBERTa-base, multilingual). Pass ``meta-llama/Llama-Prompt-Guard-2-22M`` for the smaller English-only variant with ~75% lower latency.
prompt_guard.name: model_id

protectai.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``ProtectAI/deberta-v3-small-prompt-injection-v2``. Pass one of the other entries (e.g. ``"ProtectAI/deberta-v3-base-prompt-injection-v2"``) for a larger DeBERTa-v3 classifier, or ``"ProtectAI/distilroberta-base-rejection-v1"`` for the rejection head.
protectai.name: model_id

qwen3_guard.description: If ``True`` (default), only ``Safe`` verdicts pass validation; set ``False`` to let ``Controversial`` content pass (``valid=True``), leaving it reflected only in ``score`` and ``extra["severity"]``.
qwen3_guard.name: strict

qwen3_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID, one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``Qwen/Qwen3Guard-Gen-0.6B``.
qwen3_guard.name: model_id

qwen3_guard_stream.description: If ``True`` (default), only ``Safe`` verdicts pass validation; set ``False`` to let ``Controversial`` content pass (``valid=True``), leaving it reflected only in ``score``, ``extra``, and ``spans``.
qwen3_guard_stream.name: strict

qwen3_guard_stream.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``Qwen/Qwen3Guard-Stream-0.6B``.
qwen3_guard_stream.name: model_id

qwen3_guard_stream.description: Optional assistant response moderated token-by-token, e.g. ``"I can't help with that."``. When supplied, flagged token runs are returned as ``spans`` with character offsets into this text; when omitted, only the prompt is moderated and no spans are produced.
qwen3_guard_stream.name: output_text

selene.description: The score rubric applied to every ``validate`` call — the evaluation objective plus ``Score 1:`` … ``Score 5:`` descriptions, e.g. ``"How helpful is the answer? Score 1: not helpful. ... Score 5: fully helpful."``.
selene.name: rubric

selene.description: The score (1-5) at or above which the response passes (or at or below when ``higher_is_better=False``), e.g. ``4``.
selene.name: pass_threshold

selene.description: Whether higher rubric scores mean better responses. Set ``False`` for rubrics where a higher number is worse. Defaults to ``True``.
selene.name: higher_is_better

selene.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``AtlaAI/Selene-1-Mini-Llama-3.1-8B``.
selene.name: model_id

selene.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (must fill ``{instruction}`` / ``{response}`` / ``{rubric}``). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
selene.name: prompt

selene.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
selene.name: prompt_version

selene.description: The response being graded against the rubric, e.g. ``"The article argues that remote work boosts productivity."``. When ``None``, ``input_text`` itself is graded as the response.
selene.name: output_text

sentinel.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to (and currently only supports) ``qualifire/prompt-injection-sentinel``.
sentinel.name: model_id

shield_gemma.description: The free-text safety principle the prompt is judged against, inserted into ShieldGemma's judgment template as ``{safety_policy}``. Bring-your-own policy — e.g. ``"No Hate Speech: The prompt shall not contain or seek generation of content that targets identity or protected attributes ..."``.
shield_gemma.name: policy

shield_gemma.description: Decision threshold on the ``Yes`` (violation) probability. ``valid`` is ``score < threshold``; raise it to flag only higher-confidence violations, lower it to be stricter. Defaults to ``0.5``.
shield_gemma.name: threshold

shield_gemma.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID; must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``. Defaults to ``google/shieldgemma-2b``.
shield_gemma.name: model_id

shield_gemma.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (must fill ``{user_prompt}`` and ``{safety_policy}``). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
shield_gemma.name: prompt

shield_gemma.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
shield_gemma.name: prompt_version

watsonx_guardian.description: watsonx.ai region endpoint, e.g. ``https://us-south.ml.cloud.ibm.com``. If ``None``, it is read from ``WATSONX_URL``. Ignored when ``api_client`` is supplied.
watsonx_guardian.name: url

watsonx_guardian.description: watsonx project ID. If ``None``, it is read from ``WATSONX_PROJECT_ID``. One of ``project_id`` / ``space_id`` is required (unless ``api_client`` is supplied).
watsonx_guardian.name: project_id

watsonx_guardian.description: watsonx deployment space ID. If ``None``, it is read from ``WATSONX_SPACE_ID``. Alternative to ``project_id``.
watsonx_guardian.name: space_id

watsonx_guardian.description: Detector configuration forwarded to ``Guardian``. Defaults to ``{"granite_guardian": {}}``; pass e.g. ``{"granite_guardian": {"threshold": 0.6}, "pii": {}}`` to tune thresholds or add the ``hap`` / ``pii`` detectors.
watsonx_guardian.name: detectors

watsonx_guardian.description: A pre-built ``ibm_watsonx_ai.APIClient``. When supplied, the credential arguments above are ignored and the client is used as-is (useful for shared clients or testing).
watsonx_guardian.name: api_client

wild_guard.description: Optional HuggingFace model ID. Must be one of ``SUPPORTED_MODELS``; defaults to ``allenai/wildguard``.
wild_guard.name: model_id

wild_guard.description: Optional prompt-template override, used as-is (must fill ``{prompt}`` / ``{response}``). Defaults to ``None`` — the registry default, or the version named by ``prompt_version``.
wild_guard.name: prompt

wild_guard.description: Registered prompt version to use when ``prompt`` is not given. Defaults to ``None`` (the default version). See ``AnyGuardrail.list_prompt_versions``.
wild_guard.name: prompt_version

wild_guard.description: Optional assistant response judged alongside the request, e.g. ``"I can't help with that."``. When omitted, only request harm is evaluated and the response-side signals may be absent.
wild_guard.name: output_text
